ResumeSection.title: SUMMARY
ResumeSection.Bullet: Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science (University Honors) and Minor in Machine Learning
4 years non-internship professional experience using C, C++, Python in full software development life cycle with Agile methodologies and Azure DevOps
Strong background in statistics, deep learning (PyTorch, Keras), and natural language processing

Skill.FormatOneLine: Languages: C/C++, Python, CUDA, TypeScript, JavaScript, Java, HLSL, HTML, CSS
Frameworks & Tools: PyTorch, TensorFlow, DirectML, DirectX, Tailwind, Vite, Deno
Data & Backend: Postgres, SQLite, Supabase, Cosmos, Kusto, Azure DevOps

ExperienceItem.Bullet: Worked on the Windows DirectML library which turns Machine Learning (ML) workloads from libraries like ONNX and TensorFlow into optimized pipelines for accelerators like GPU and NPU
Implemented metacommands that enhance the GPU driver support for different vendors
Set up 10 test machines for in-house testing of the DirectML library across multiple GPU vendors
Maintained the C++ codebase via regression testing and analyzing the library code using Windbg and the DirectX HLSL shaders using PIX
Implemented C++ data conversion functions and the logic for the public WinRT API of a secure, short-range object communication protocol for embedded IoT devices following IEEE specifications
Established the groundwork for fuzz testing of LUI APIs
Maintained the C++ codebase via regression testing and analyzing the library code in Windbg
Developed a configurable, extendable, and exhaustive test suite in Python for a new smart IoT sensor
Developed the backend of a prototype feature in Microsoft Defender using Cosmos and Kusto, Microsoft’s in-house big data solution
Worked in image segmentation for use in machine perception, improving upon the state-of-the-art computer vision algorithm ENet
Developed a technique for finding flaws in training datasets that improves inference performance, implemented in PyTorch
Developed an application in C/C++ to detect a machine's state and measure its payload via telemetry
Developed a debug tool in Python to simulate multiple Electronic Control Modules, eliminating the need to wait for a full test bench
Led the efforts to create auto-graded unit tests and a better template for the Backpropagation programming assignment
Led recitations on Vector Differentiation for 30 students.

ProjectItem.Bullet: Technologies used: Vite, Typescript, Tailwind, Supabase
Created a gamified skill training web app for artists and musicians.
Leveraged AI agents to accelerate the whole software development life cycle.

Header.ProjectName: Senior Thesis: Exploiting Extra Inputs During Training Time

ProjectItem.Bullet: Investigated the theoretical basis of when to perform imputation for multimodal ML tasks
Proved linear distribution empirical averaging is superior to prediction during test time

Header.ProjectName: Autoencoder-Based Attacks on Deep Learning Models

ProjectItem.Bullet: Successfully attacked an ENet image segmentation model, resulting in 3x decrease in IoU score.

Header.ProjectName: Dense CNN for Chest Disease Identification

ProjectItem.Bullet: Achieved better AUROC scores than CheXNet model. Won "Best in Show" at Hack Auton.
Developed automatic food inventory system using CV in TensorFlow.

Header.ProjectName: Sight2Sound, an Aid to the Blind

ProjectItem.Bullet: Transformed videos into AM audio by mapping pixels to frequencies using the Hilbert curve.

ResumeSection.title: EDUCATION
ResumeSection.Bullet: B.S. in Computer Science (University Honors), Minor in Machine Learning

ResumeSection.title: PUBLICATIONS
ResumeSection.Bullet: X. Guan, J. Lee, P. Wu, Y. Wu. Machine Learning for Exam Triage. HackAuton, 2018.
S.Kazadi, G. Jeno, X. Guan, N. Nusgart, A. Sheptunov. Decision making swarms. MAICS Conference, 2017.
